The LLM identifies a 40% loss of informative text, specifically critical financial data and list items, which is corroborated by the programmatic shingling recall dropping below the 0.75 threshold.

concerns.analysis: The reading order for items 5 through 12 is significantly corrupted. In the source, each topic is immediately followed by its presenter on the same line (columns). In the output JSON, all the topics for items 6-13 are grouped into a single list (reading_order 13), and then all the presenters are listed as individual paragraphs (reading_order 14-20) afterward. This disconnects the topics from their presenters, making the document logically incoherent for screen reader users.

concerns.output_quote: The list of topics (reading_order 13) is completed before the names of the presenters (reading_order 14-20) begin.

concerns.analysis: The output fails to capture significant portions of the list structure. Specifically: 1) The financial details (Fund Balance, Disbursement) under items 4b, 4c, 4d, and 4e are entirely omitted from the output. 2) The nested sub-items i. through v. under item 4g are missing. 3) The sub-items a) through f) under item 5 are missing. This is a major failure in preserving the hierarchical list structure of the agenda.

concerns.analysis: In the source PDF, agenda items 5 through 12 are organized in a clear two-column table/layout with the 'Topic' on the left and the 'Presenter' on the right. This allows immediate visual association between an item and its speaker. In the HTML rendering, this organization is lost; the topics are listed sequentially (items 6-13), followed by a completely separate list of names at the bottom of the section. In the HTML screenshot, these items are reduced to simple list headers with no underlying content. This is a critical failure of visual fidelity as significant structured data has been omitted.
